title: artifact-graph per program and pack
purpose: C4.4 defines artifact graph per program and pack for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery.
scope: Owns `C4.4` only; siblings under `C4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §5 — Branch C — Product execution plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#5-branch-c-product-execution-plane).
behavior_steps: When S3 architecture ambiguity resolves during pursuit, the conductor dual-writes the session decision to journal Resolved Q&A and persists durable rationale as an ADR under `docs/decisions/` per [B1.4](B1.4-s3-architecture-ambiguity.md).

ADRs record architecture choices, gate waivers with rationale, and program manifest conflicts—repo-authored judgment distinct from external truth in [E3.1](E3.1-facts-docs-facts-external-truth.md) facts files.

Design artifacts and task cards link to settled ADRs when implement work depends on a recorded choice so workers load authoritative rationale via `allowed_reads` without re-litigating the trade-off in chat.
H1-approved plans with open S3 trade-offs cannot spawn implement workers until the conductor files an ADR or documents the waiver in journal with explicit operator visibility.

When an ADR supersedes prior assumptions, [E5.1](E5.1-staleness-design-graph-staleness-json.md) staleness marks downstream design nodes and pursuit pauses at H2 until reconcile-stale or operator confirmation updates the artifact graph.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `C4.4` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `C4.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `C4.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `C4.4` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.
Create or extend S0 script if behavior is file-derived.
Add unit test under tests/unit/test_c4_4.py when script exists.
Validate `C4.4` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `C4.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `C4.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: C4.4 defines artifact graph per program and pack for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery. In practice, the system when s3 architecture ambiguity resolves during pursuit, the conductor dual-writes the session decision to journal resolved q&a and persists durable rationale as an adr under `docs/decisions/` per [b1.4](b1.4-s3-architecture-ambiguity.md), then adrs record architecture choices, gate waivers with rationale, and program manifest conflicts—repo-authored judgment distinct from external truth in [e3.1](e3.1-facts-docs-facts-external-truth.md) facts files.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-c 2026-06-28 -->

Artifact graph nodes represent design, code, test, and pack artifacts with dependency edges for program and pack scopes. reconcile-artifact-graph marks stale when upstream changes; pursuit respects stale flags before spawn.

Graph complements staleness.json for platform nodes; together they prevent implement against obsolete design. Pack schema may embed graph templates per company type.
